classes.name: Barbarian
classes.description: A fierce warrior of primitive background who can enter a battle rage. Barbarians are fierce warriors from the wilds. They live for the thrill of battle and revel in the joy of conquest.

subclasses.name: Path of the Berserker
subclasses.description: For some barbarians, rage is a means to an end – that end being violence. The Path of the Berserker is a path of untrammeled fury, slick with blood.

subclasses.name: Path of the Totem Warrior
subclasses.description: The Path of the Totem Warrior is a spiritual journey, as the barbarian accepts a spirit animal as guide, protector, and inspiration.

subclasses.name: Path of the Ancestral Guardian
subclasses.description: Some barbarians hail from cultures that revere their ancestors. These tribes teach that the warriors of the past linger in the world as mighty spirits.

classes.name: Bard
classes.description: An inspiring magician whose power echoes the music of creation. Bards use their artistry to inspire, enchant, and unravel mysteries.

subclasses.name: College of Lore
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Lore know something about most things, collecting bits of knowledge from sources as diverse as scholarly tomes and peasant tales.

subclasses.name: College of Valor
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Valor are daring skalds whose tales keep alive the memory of the great heroes of the past.

subclasses.name: College of Swords
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Swords are called blades, and they entertain through daring feats of weapon prowess.

classes.name: Cleric
classes.description: A priestly champion who wields divine magic in service of a higher power. Clerics are intermediaries between the mortal world and the distant planes of the gods.

subclasses.name: Life Domain
subclasses.description: The Life domain focuses on the vibrant positive energy – one of the fundamental forces of the universe – that sustains all life.

subclasses.name: Light Domain
subclasses.description: Gods of light – including Helm, Lathander, Pholtus, Branchala, the Silver Flame, Belenus, Apollo, and Re-Horakhty – promote the ideals of rebirth and renewal.

subclasses.name: Trickery Domain
subclasses.description: Gods of trickery – such as Tymora, Beshaba, Olidammara, the Traveler, Garl Glittergold, and Loki – are mischief-makers and instigators.

subclasses.name: War Domain
subclasses.description: War has many manifestations. It can make heroes of ordinary people. It can be desperate and horrific, with acts of cruelty and cowardice eclipsing instances of excellence and courage.

classes.name: Druid
classes.description: A priest of the Old Faith, wielding the powers of nature – moonlight and plant growth, fire and lightning – and adopting animal forms.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Moon
subclasses.description: Druids of the Circle of the Moon are fierce guardians of the wilds. Their order gathers under the full moon to share news and trade warnings.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Land
subclasses.description: The Circle of the Land is made up of mystics and sages who safeguard ancient knowledge and rites through a vast oral tradition.

classes.name: Fighter
classes.description: A master of martial combat, skilled with a variety of weapons and armor. Fighters share an unparalleled mastery with weapons and armor.

subclasses.name: Champion
subclasses.description: The archetypal Champion focuses on the development of raw physical power honed to deadly perfection.

subclasses.name: Battle Master
subclasses.description: Those who emulate the archetypal Battle Master employ martial techniques passed down through generations.

subclasses.name: Eldritch Knight
subclasses.description: The archetypal Eldritch Knight combines the martial mastery common to all fighters with a careful study of magic.

classes.name: Monk
classes.description: A master of martial arts, harnessing the power of the body in pursuit of physical and spiritual perfection.

subclasses.name: Way of the Open Hand
subclasses.description: Monks of the Way of the Open Hand are the ultimate masters of martial arts combat, whether armed or unarmed.

subclasses.name: Way of Shadow
subclasses.description: Monks of the Way of Shadow follow a tradition that values stealth and subterfuge. These monks might be called ninjas or shadowdancers.

subclasses.name: Way of the Four Elements
subclasses.description: You follow a monastic tradition that teaches you to harness the elements. When you focus your ki, you can align yourself with the forces of creation.

classes.name: Paladin
classes.description: A holy warrior bound to a sacred oath. Paladins are united by their oaths to stand against the forces of evil.

subclasses.name: Oath of Devotion
subclasses.description: The Oath of Devotion binds a paladin to the loftiest ideals of justice, virtue, and order.

subclasses.name: Oath of the Ancients
subclasses.description: The Oath of the Ancients is as old as the race of elves and the rituals of the druids. Sometimes called fey knights or green knights.

subclasses.name: Oath of Vengeance
subclasses.description: The Oath of Vengeance is a solemn commitment to punish those who have committed a grievous sin.

classes.name: Ranger
classes.description: A warrior who uses martial prowess and nature magic to combat threats on the edges of civilization.

subclasses.name: Hunter
subclasses.description: Emulating the Hunter archetype means accepting your place as a bulwark between civilization and the terrors of the wilderness.

subclasses.name: Beast Master
subclasses.description: The Beast Master archetype embodies a friendship between the civilized races and the beasts of the world.

classes.name: Rogue
classes.description: A scoundrel who uses stealth and trickery to overcome obstacles and enemies. Rogues have many faces: a swindler, bounty hunter, or guild thief.

subclasses.name: Thief
subclasses.description: You hone your skills in the larcenous arts. Burglars, bandits, cutpurses, and other criminals typically follow this archetype.

subclasses.name: Assassin
subclasses.description: You focus your training on the grim art of death. Those who adhere to this archetype are diverse: hired killers, spies, bounty hunters.

subclasses.name: Arcane Trickster
subclasses.description: Some rogues enhance their fine-honed skills of stealth and agility with magic, learning tricks of enchantment and illusion.

classes.name: Sorcerer
classes.description: A spellcaster who draws on inherent magic from a gift or bloodline. Sorcerers carry a magical birthright conferred upon them by an exotic bloodline.

subclasses.name: Draconic Bloodline
subclasses.description: Your innate magic comes from draconic magic that was mingled with your blood or that of your ancestors.

subclasses.name: Wild Magic
subclasses.description: Your innate magic comes from the wild forces of chaos that underlie the order of creation.

classes.name: Warlock
classes.description: A wielder of magic that is derived from a bargain with an extraplanar entity. Warlocks are seekers of the knowledge that lies hidden in the fabric of the multiverse.

subclasses.name: The Archfey
subclasses.description: Your patron is a lord or lady of the fey, a creature of legend who holds secrets that were forgotten before the mortal races were born.

subclasses.name: The Fiend
subclasses.description: You have made a pact with a fiend from the lower planes of existence, a being whose aims are evil, even if you strive against those aims.

subclasses.name: The Great Old One
subclasses.description: Your patron is a mysterious entity whose nature is utterly foreign to the fabric of reality. It has granted you magic that twists and distorts reality.

classes.name: Wizard
classes.description: A scholarly magic-user capable of manipulating the structures of reality. Wizards are supreme magic-users, defined and united as a class by the spells they cast.

subclasses.name: School of Abjuration
subclasses.description: The School of Abjuration emphasizes magic that blocks, banishes, or protects. Detractors of this school say that its tradition is about denial, negation rather than positive assertion.

subclasses.name: School of Conjuration
subclasses.description: As a conjurer, you favor spells that produce objects and creatures out of thin air. You can conjure billowing clouds of daggers.

subclasses.name: School of Divination
subclasses.description: The counsel of a diviner is sought by royalty and commoners alike, for all seek a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future.

subclasses.name: School of Enchantment
subclasses.description: As a member of the School of Enchantment, you have honed your ability to magically entrance and beguile other people and monsters.

subclasses.name: School of Evocation
subclasses.description: You focus your study on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid.

subclasses.name: School of Illusion
subclasses.description: You focus your studies on magic that dazzles the senses, befuddles the mind, and tricks even the wisest folk.

subclasses.name: School of Necromancy
subclasses.description: The School of Necromancy explores the cosmic forces of life, death, and undeath. As you focus your studies in this tradition, you learn to manipulate the energy that animates all living things.

subclasses.name: School of Transmutation
subclasses.description: You are a student of spells that modify energy and matter. To you, the world is not a fixed thing, but eminently mutable.
